title: Partnership inquiry from MyClaw.ai
author: LeoYeAI
body:
Hi,

OpenClaw-bot-review — lightweight web dashboard for viewing OpenClaw Bots/Agents — is a clean utility. 1.9K stars.

MyClaw.ai is managed cloud hosting for OpenClaw. Open to a partnership?

Leo | MyClaw.ai

title: 会自动闪退？

body: 会自动闪退？

title: Possible complementary direction: SkillClaw as the backend skill lifecycle layer
author: Upper9527
body:
Hi, I'd like to suggest a complementary direction for `OpenClaw-bot-review`.

Project:
https://github.com/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw

A dashboard for reviewing bots / agents / sessions solves the visibility side. SkillClaw solves a different long-term problem: the skill libraries behind those agents become duplicated, stale, and fragmented over time.

Its role is a post-task skill evolution loop that deduplicates, merges, improves, and shares skills across agents/devices/teams.

I think the two directions are complementary rather than overlapping.

Paper:
https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08377

title: bug: stats summary cards don't change when switching time range tabs
author: roosehash
body:
## Problem

On the stats page, the summary cards (Input Tokens, Output Tokens, Total Messages) show the same values regardless of which time range tab (Daily / Weekly / Monthly) is selected.

Currently all three tabs show the **all-time total**, which isn't useful — users expect:
- **Daily tab** → today's stats
- **Weekly tab** → this week's stats  
- **Monthly tab** → this month's stats

## Root Cause

In `app/stats/page.tsx`, the totals are calculated from `currentData` which contains **all** data points, just grouped differently:

```typescript
const currentData = stats[range]; // daily/weekly/monthly
const totalInput = currentData.reduce((s, d) => s + d.inputTokens, 0);  // sums ALL entries
```

Since `weekly` and `monthly` are aggregated from the same `daily` data (in the API route), the sum is always identical.

## Expected Behavior

Summary cards should reflect the **current period only**:
- Daily: sum of today's data point only
- Weekly: sum of the current week's data point only
- Monthly: sum of the current month's data point only

## Suggested Fix

Filter `currentData` to the latest entry before summing:

```typescript
const latestEntry = currentData[currentData.length - 1];
const totalInput = latestEntry?.inputTokens ?? 0;
const totalOutput = latestEntry?.outputTokens ?? 0;
const totalMessages = latestEntry?.messageCount ?? 0;
```

Or add a date range filter so users can see any specific period.

title: Bug: 自動刷新間隔 (refreshInterval) 設定不會保存到 localStorage
author: jlin53882
body:
## Bug 描述

設定自動刷新間隔（10秒、30秒、1分鐘等）後，當下會正常運作，但**重新整理網頁後就會還原成預設值（手動）**。

## 問題原因

`app/page.tsx` 中有儲存其他狀態到 localStorage：
- `agentTestResults`
- `platformTestResults`  
- `sessionTestResults`
- `dmSessionResults`

但**漏掉了** `refreshInterval`（自動刷新間隔）的儲存和讀取。

## 修復建議

1. 首次載入時從 localStorage 恢復 `refreshInterval`
2. 每次更改時儲存到 localStorage

## 影響範圍

所有使用者都會遇到這個問題，導致自動刷新功能無法正常使用。

---
報告者：@jiahao

title: 服务运行配置增加BasePath设定
author: mvptianyu
body:
RT，部份场景可能部署于服务后端机器环境，前置通过网关/nginx等代理路由。而当前程序启动基本依赖/的根路径URL规则（包括路由/API/静态资源等），路由/静态资源可通过next配置解决，但API接口调用貌似不行。

综上，建议这里可以考虑拓展支持BasePath自定义设置，方便部署和URL自定义转发规则配置。

title: 建议增加监控多个openclaw 的配置，另外报个 BUG
author: millsguo
body:
现在只能配置一个openclaw，希望以后能增加多个openclaw 的监控。

在读.openclaw/agents 下的目录时，需要过滤掉@eaDir，这个目录是 Synology 群晖NAS 系统中一个用于存储元数据的隐藏系统文件夹，你把他也当一个agent了。

title: 是否可以改下仓库的名称
author: HaydnLiao
body:
这个大小写看着太难受了，命名方式没有遵循任何一种规范，建议全小写。
要么按readme改成：openclaw-dashboard；
或者是网页上的：openclaw-bot-dashboard；
或者是：openclaw-monitor/clawboard/clawoffice等。

title: Add `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` to standardize bug reports and feature requests
author: KlementMultiverse
body:
## Problem

The repository currently has no issue template, making it difficult for contributors to submit well-structured bug reports and feature requests. Looking at existing issues (#21, #20, #16), they vary widely in detail and structure — some lack reproduction steps, others mix multiple concerns in a single issue. This creates friction for maintainers during triage and makes it harder for other contributors to understand what's being asked.

## Why it matters

With 13 open issues and growing community interest (agents across Feishu, Discord, etc.), a standardized template would:

1. **Reduce triage time** — Maintainers get reproducible steps, config context, and expected behavior upfront instead of asking for clarification
2. **Prevent duplicate issues** — Clear sections help contributors search for existing work before opening new issues
3. **Model best practices** — New contributors see the expected format immediately when opening an issue
4. **Improve issue quality** — When creating an issue for bot state sync (#21) or remote server connection (#20), contributors would naturally include their OpenClaw config version, platform bindings, and reproduction steps

## What good looks like

GitHub's `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` folder should contain:

1. **`bug_report.md`** — For bugs like #21 (bot state not syncing in multi-bot scenarios)
   - Steps to reproduce (how many bots, which platforms, what state change)
   - Expected vs actual behavior
   - OpenClaw config excerpt (sanitized)
   - Dashboard version, browser, OS

2. **`feature_request.md`** — For requests like #20 (remote server connection) and #16 (login feature)
   - Use case (why is this needed)
   - Proposed behavior (what should happen)
   - Related OpenClaw features or external integrations
   - Acceptance criteria

3. **`config.yml`** — Route to appropriate templates when issue is opened

## Implementation

Create three files:

- `docs/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md` — 25 lines with reproducible steps
- `docs/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md` — 20 lines with use case + acceptance criteria
- `docs/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` — Route issue types

Reference similar dashboards (Grafana, Portainer) that use templates for consistency.

## Priority

Low — nice-to-have quality improvement that scales with contributor volume. Can be completed in under 1 hour.


---
*Contributed by [Klement Gunndu](https://github.com/KlementMultiverse)*
